tier_3_findings.finding: Placement deviation in 3 agents (test-engineer, e2e-test-engineer, backlog-creator): canonical NEVER bullet is placed BEFORE the Block Comment Template fence (immediately preceding the 'On failure:' block), not as the last bullet of ## Constraints. Spec mandates 'last bullet of ## Constraints' for all agents except sprint-planner and publisher (which are correctly placed post-fence). The bullet IS present and functionally correct — this is ordering, not absence.
tier_3_findings.evidence: agents/test-engineer.md:56 (NEVER bullet), line 57 (On failure: Block...). Same pattern at e2e-test-engineer.md:74-75, backlog-creator.md:93-94.
tier_3_findings.recommendation: For consistency with the placement contract, move the NEVER bullet to the absolute last position in ## Constraints (after the Block Comment Template fence) in these 3 agents, matching the sprint-planner + publisher pattern.

tier_3_findings.finding: Anti-pattern gate (v6.10.0-no-awk-source-in-rewrites.sh) uses a narrow regex — 'awk[[:space:]].*\^.*\\(\\)' — that only matches awk programs containing literal ^ and \(\). A malicious future PR could slip in: 'awk "/pattern/,/end/" file.sh > /tmp/x.sh && . /tmp/x.sh' which has no ^ or \(\) and would not be detected. The gate is not vacuous (negative control verifies the specific pattern), but it is narrower than its stated intent ('awk extracting function to .sh file AND dot-sourcing it').
tier_3_findings.evidence: tests/scenarios/v6.10.0-no-awk-source-in-rewrites.sh lines 63-72 — regex pattern 'awk[[:space:]].*\^.*\\(\\)' only matches function-extraction awk, not range-based extraction.
tier_3_findings.recommendation: Broaden the awk detection regex to also catch 'awk "/pattern/,/end/"' style extractions combined with sourcing, or add a second check for any shell source ('. *.sh') of non-fixtures files alongside any awk call in the same scenario file.

tier_3_findings.finding: No explicit parse_pause_timeout exemption in the anti-pattern gate. The gate's self-exclusion comment notes 'Exception: sourcing tests/lib/fixtures.sh (permitted per REQ-T1-6)' but the pause-timeout scenario (v6.9.0-pause-timeout-validation.sh) uses inline reimplementation per REQ-T1-5 path (a) — explicitly NOT awk-extracted. This is safe by design, but the gate contains no allowlist entry noting this fact. The allowlist is implicit (the regex simply doesn't match inline-redefine form), not explicit. A future maintainer might not understand why the scenario passes.
tier_3_findings.evidence: tests/scenarios/v6.9.0-pause-timeout-validation.sh lines 4, 21, 23 — comments document REQ-T1-5 path (a) compliance, but the gate file itself has no corresponding comment.
tier_3_findings.recommendation: Add a comment in the gate file acknowledging parse_pause_timeout inline reimplementation as the REQ-T1-5 path (a) compliant case that does not trigger the awk+source pattern.

check_1_track3_prompt_injection.notes: All 11 target agents have the canonical NEVER bullet byte-identical to agents/code-analyst.md:120. Placement is correct for 8 of 11: last bullet (spec-reviewer, spec-writer, rollback-agent, scaffolder, stack-selector, deployment-verifier) and post-Block-Comment-Template-fence (sprint-planner, publisher). Three agents (test-engineer, e2e-test-engineer, backlog-creator) have the bullet immediately before rather than after their Block Comment fence — minor placement deviation, bullet is present and effective.

check_2_hook_script.notes: No eval present. No unquoted $() with user-controlled variables. STAGES array is hardcoded — never derived from state.json field names. All jq calls redirect stderr to /dev/null. Exit 0 always. Audit log is plain-text 3-field append-only. set -uo pipefail present (NOT -e). The $stage variable in jq path '.${stage}.dispatched_at' is sourced exclusively from the hardcoded STAGES array — no injection sink. $stdin_json is passed to jq via printf pipe (safe). $STATE_JSON is double-quoted in jq call (safe for word splitting). CEOS_AUDIT_LOG env override uses >> redirection (file creation only, no command injection).

check_3_regression.notes: All 10 previously-patched agents retain canonical bullet byte-identical (confirmed via git grep on forge/v6.10.0). prompt-injection-protection.sh uses find-based enumeration (not hardcoded array), counts exactly 21 agents, and includes regression check for all 10 pre-patched agents. Cross-file invariants: License SPDX 'MIT' consistent across plugin.json (blob c2fce39), marketplace.json (blob a6616a7), LICENSE first line. Maintainer email filip.sabacky@ceosdata.com present in SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md; CONTRIBUTING.md has no match (CONTRIBUTING.md does not currently carry the maintainer email — but invariant #2 requires it in all three). Template byte-parity: .gitea bug_report.md == .github bug_report.md (both blob baa712c); feature_request.md pair (both 0308124); PR template pair (both c9fc6a2).

check_4_residual_risk.notes: core/agent-states.md contains explicit 'Tracker content normalization — deferred' section at line 93, citing T3-ADV-1/T3-ADV-2/T3-ADV-3 with NOT CLOSED status and v6.11.0 deferral. docs/plans/roadmap.md v6.11.0 entry (item 4) explicitly names 'Prompt-injection defense-in-depth (T3-ADV-1/ADV-2/ADV-3 deferred)' as motivation. docs/guides/dispatch-enforcement.md contains 'Known limitation: Autopilot subprocess dispatch audit gap' section (lines 120-133) confirming T2-ADV-3 autopilot audit parity is a v6.10.1 follow-up — disclosure is present and specific.

check_5_antipattern_gate.notes: Gate covers all 50 V6100_TOUCHED files (cardinality-verified). Negative control (synthetic awk+source fixture) confirms the gate is non-vacuous. Self-exclusion uses -ef file comparison (scoped to gate file only, not a broad bypass). parse_pause_timeout exemption is implicit (regex simply doesn't match inline-redefine form) rather than explicit allowlist. MAJOR gap: regex 'awk[[:space:]].*\^.*\\(\\)' only catches function-extraction awk patterns (requiring ^ and \(\) in the awk program). A range-based awk extraction like 'awk "/start/,/end/" f.sh > /tmp/x.sh && . /tmp/x.sh' in a TOUCHED file would slip past the gate undetected.
